workflows.title: Verified Erdős 64 prefix

stages.title: Official Erdős 64 statement
stages.summary: The pinned public boundary asks for a power-of-two cycle in every finite graph of minimum degree at least three.

primaryDeclaration.type: Prop
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.OfficialStatement

stages.title: Executable internal problem
stages.summary: The same Mathlib graph is paired with an explicit finite inspection order and an executable but equivalent length predicate.

primaryDeclaration.type: (V : Type u) → StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V fun _object => Unit
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.staticInput

evidenceDeclarations.type: (P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem) → (P.Ambient → Prop) → P.Ambient → Prop
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.IsCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} {G : P.Ambient},
  ¬StructuralExhaustion.Core.IsCounterexample P Target G → P.Baseline G → Target G
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.target_of_not_isCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type u} → Internal.Object V → Prop
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.IsCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  ¬Internal.IsCounterexample object →
    Internal.Baseline object → ∃ exponent vertex cycle, exponent ≥ 2 ∧ cycle.IsCycle ∧ cycle.length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.officialConclusion_of_notCounterexample

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ (length : ℕ), Internal.PowerOfTwoLength length ↔ ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.powerOfTwoLength_iff

stages.title: CT1 Mersenne target algebra
stages.summary: CT1 validates one rooted return or constructs the exact avoiding residual from disjoint return sets.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (object : Internal.Object V) →
    (baseline : Internal.Baseline object) →
      ¬Internal.Target object →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT1.CertifiedAvoidingRun (Internal.ct1Spec V) (Internal.ct1Input object baseline)
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runAvoidingCT1

evidenceDeclarations.type: ∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V), Internal.Target object ↔ Internal.HasMersenneReturn object.graph
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.target_iff_hasMersenneReturn

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (object : Internal.Object V),
  ¬Internal.Target object ↔
    ∀ (dart : object.graph.Dart), Disjoint (Internal.returnSet object.graph dart) Internal.MersenneSet
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.not_target_iff_returnSets_disjoint

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{Residual : Type uResidual} →
  {Previous : Residual → Type uPrevious} →
    (family : StructuralExhaustion.CT1.ResidualRefinement.DependentCertificateFamily Residual Previous) →
      (∀ (residual : Residual) (previous : Previous residual),
          ¬family.PublicTarget residual previous (family.input residual previous).context.G) →
        {facts : List (Residual → Prop)} →
          [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.Proofs.Contains
                (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available family.DecisionSuccessor) facts] →
            StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.StageNode family.AvoidingSuccessor

stages.title: CT2 no proper core
stages.summary: A supplied proper finite subgraph is one certified local reduction; target transport and lexicographic minimality force its minimum degree to be at most two.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (residual : Internal.InitialResidual V) →
    ((StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.initial residual).add Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail
          ⋯).BranchResult
      [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available fun current => Internal.Node8Stage current,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node7Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node6Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node5Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node4Output,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.Yes Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail]
      [StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.Closed Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.No Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail]
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runInitialThroughNode8

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{V : Type u} →
  {facts : List (Internal.InitialResidual V → Prop)} →
    [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.Proofs.Contains
          (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available fun current => Internal.Node7Stage current)
          facts] →
      StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.StageNode fun current => Internal.Node8Stage current
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.node8NoProperCore

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {residual : Internal.InitialResidual V} (node7 : Internal.Node7Stage residual)
  (output : Internal.Node8Output node7)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph output.context.G),
  ¬Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.Baseline subgraph.value
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.node8_noProperCore

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} {residual : Internal.InitialResidual V} (node7 : Internal.Node7Stage residual)
  (output : Internal.Node8Output node7)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph output.context.G)
  (baseline : Internal.packedStaticInput.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  (Internal.packedStaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run output.context subgraph baseline).terminal =
      StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2 ∧
    (Internal.packedStaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run output.context subgraph baseline).trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal] ∧
      (Internal.packedStaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run output.context subgraph baseline).checks = 1
evidenceDeclarations.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.node8_ct2_certificate

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  ∃ run,
    run.terminal = StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2 ∧
      run.trace =
        [StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.entry, StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionDecision,
          StructuralExhaustion.CT2.Graph.NodeId.deletionC2Terminal]
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_total

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target)
  (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G)
  (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value),
  (input.properSubgraphCT2Run ctx subgraph baseline).checks ≤
    (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).coefficient *
      ((StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).size
            (input.properSubgraphCT2Input ctx subgraph baseline) +
          1) ^
        (StructuralExhaustion.CT2.certifiedReductionBudget ctx).degree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run_polynomial

evidenceDeclarations.type: {V : Type u} → Internal.InitialResidual V → Type (u + 1)

stages.title: Routed CT2 deletion criticality
stages.summary: The certified CT1 residual enters local CT2 discovery; its exact disabled result forces every edge to have a degree-three endpoint.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (residual : Internal.InitialResidual V) →
    ((StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.initial residual).add Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail
          ⋯).BranchResult
      [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node9Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available fun current => Internal.Node8Stage current,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node7Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node6Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node5Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node4Output,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.Yes Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail]
      [StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.Closed Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.No Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail]
primaryDeclaration.name: Erdos64EG.Internal.runInitialThroughNode9

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput)
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target),
  (∀ (dart : ctx.G.object.graph.Dart),
      ctx.G.object.degree dart.toProd.1 = input.minimumDegree ∨
        ctx.G.object.degree dart.toProd.2 = input.minimumDegree) →
    ∀ {left right : ctx.G.Vertex},
      input.minimumDegree + 1 ≤ ctx.G.object.degree left →
        input.minimumDegree + 1 ≤ ctx.G.object.degree right → ¬ctx.G.object.graph.Adj left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.slackVerticesIndependent_of_tightEndpoint

stages.title: CT3 boundaried replacement
stages.summary: Literal packed-graph CT3 gluing derives whole-rank decrease, minimum-degree preservation, and target transport from local replacement data.

primaryDeclaration.type:
{V : Type u} →
  (residual : Internal.InitialResidual V) →
    ((StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.initial residual).add Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail
          ⋯).BranchResult
      [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node11Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node10Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node9Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available fun current => Internal.Node8Stage current,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node7Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node6Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node5Stage,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available Internal.Node4Output,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.Yes Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail]
      [StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.Closed Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        StructuralExhaustion.Core.CounterexampleBranch.No Internal.InitialResidual.object,
        Internal.InitialLargeEnoughTail]

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        left ≠
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        right →
    ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
        left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.boundaryDegreeProfile_ne_not_targetComplete

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  {left right : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T},
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries left
      right →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ContextEquivalent input boundaries
      left right
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.targetComplete_contextUniversal

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) {replacement source : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T}
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  replacement.LexSmaller source →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries replacement outside).lexRank <
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries source outside).lexRank
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_lexRank_lt

evidenceDeclarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) [Nonempty T] (threshold : ℕ)
  (source replacement : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  (∀ (boundary : T),
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries replacement boundary =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries source boundary) →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries threshold replacement →
      threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph source outside).minDegree →
        threshold ≤ (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glueGraph replacement outside).minDegree
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_preserves_minDegree

evidenceDeclarations.type:
(input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) →
  {T : Type u} →
    (boundaries : FinEnum T) →
      {ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target} →
        {atom :
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom input boundaries
              ctx} →
          (piece : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T) →
            StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input
                boundaries piece atom.source →
              ¬input.Target (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.pack boundaries piece) →
                StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries input.minimumDegree
                    piece →
                  piece.LexSmaller atom.source →
                    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression input
                      boundaries atom
evidenceDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.ofTargetComplete
StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.impossible

evidenceDeclarations.type:
{Residual : Type uResidual} →
  {facts : List (Residual → Prop)} →
    {Bypass : Residual → Type uInput} →
      {Active : Residual → Type uTarget} →
        {yes no : (residual : Residual) → Active residual → Prop} →
          {Output : (residual : Residual) → (data : Active residual) → no residual data → Sort uStage} →
            {nextYes nextNo :
                (residual : Residual) →
                  StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive Active no
                      Output residual →
                    Prop} →
              [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.Proofs.Contains
                    (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available
                      (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuation Bypass
                        Active yes no Output))
                    facts] →
                ((residual : Residual) →
                    (active :
                        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive
                          Active no Output residual) →
                      Decidable (nextYes residual active)) →
                  (∀ (residual : Residual)
                      (active :
                        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive
                          Active no Output residual),
                      ¬nextYes residual active → nextNo residual active) →
                    StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.StageNode
                      (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecision
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationBypass
                          Bypass Active yes no)
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive
                          Active no Output)
                        nextYes nextNo)

example.title: Erdős Problem 64
example.summary: A partial proof with exact Mersenne returns, lexicographic minimal selection, CT2 criticality and bridge contraction, CT3 boundaried uncompressibility, an HSS-forced induced-P13 CT1 stage, a routed maximum-packing CT12 remainder stage, the complete CT10 P13 attachment-label algebra, a polynomial ordered CT6 degree-surplus ledger with exact per-slot activation, the CT15 baseline and free/blocked pair-response stages, the exact CT9 capacity-token partition, and the exact 25-role coupled overload/class decision, together with the framework-native local-port and Type B ledgers retained by the same selected graph.

proofSteps.workBound: Statement boundary only; no computation is performed.
proofSteps.title: Official theorem boundary
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The public statement is exact, but the current verified workflow is explicitly a partial prefix and does not yet prove this proposition.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The public Lean proposition uses the same finite simple graph and asks for exactly the power-of-two cycle asserted by the manuscript's main theorem.

declarationGroups.title: Public mathematical statement
declarationGroups.explanation: This declaration is the exact Mathlib-facing proposition that the completed formalization must prove.

declarations.type: Prop
declarations.name: Erdos64EG.OfficialStatement

proofSteps.workBound: The exponent search is bounded by the candidate cycle length.
proofSteps.title: Faithful executable target
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Finite enumeration and adjacency decisions control execution order only; they do not introduce a second graph representation or weaken the theorem.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Lean equips the same Mathlib graph with finite inspection data and replaces the unbounded exponent quantifier by a bounded decidable search, then proves that this executable target is equivalent to the official conclusion.

declarationGroups.title: Encoding and equivalence bridge
declarationGroups.explanation: The static input supplies deterministic finite data; the two equivalence theorems prove that neither the cycle predicate nor its exponent range has changed.

proofSteps.workBound: One certificate check for C1 and zero checks for the proof-carrying avoiding execution.
proofSteps.title: Mersenne return target algebra
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This covers nodes [5]--[7]. The C1 edge is the manuscript target terminal; the exact avoiding edge is the sole successor consumed by node [8].
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Removing one edge from a power-of-two cycle leaves a return path whose length is one less than a power of two. Conversely, adjoining the root edge to such a return produces the target cycle. CT1 records either a supplied return or universal avoidance.
proofSteps.formalStatement: G \text{ has a } C_{2^j} \iff \exists e,\ 2^j-1 \in R_e(G)

proofSteps.title: No proper minimum-degree-three core

proofSteps.plainExplanation: If a proper subgraph still had minimum degree at least three, target avoidance would be inherited and it would be a lexicographically smaller counterexample. Certified CT2 reduction formalizes this contradiction for one arbitrary supplied proper subgraph.

declarationGroups.title: Mathematical conclusion
declarationGroups.explanation: This theorem is the direct Lean form of the manuscript lemma: every proper finite subgraph of the selected counterexample has minimum degree at most two.

proofSteps.title: Edge-deletion criticality
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is exactly nodes [9] and [10]. Each theorem is pointwise on the supplied edge or adjacent pair; no graph, path, context, state, or universe family is enumerated.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: An edge with both endpoints of degree at least four could be deleted while preserving minimum degree three, and deletion cannot create a new cycle. Minimality therefore forces every edge to touch a degree-three vertex.
proofSteps.formalStatement: xy \in E(G) \Longrightarrow d_G(x)=3 \lor d_G(y)=3;\qquad V_{\ge 4}(G) \text{ is independent}

proofSteps.workBound: Proof-level literal gluing and one certified reduction; no universe of contexts, graphs, or replacements is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Boundaried replacement and uncompressibility
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This descriptor covers only nodes [11]--[14]: boundaried atoms, context universality, replacement, and hereditary target-uncompressibility. Later rank-dependence nodes consume these facts from the ledger but are tracked by their own descriptors.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: A proper boundaried atom cannot be replaced by a strictly smaller representative with the same boundary degrees and no worse response in every compatible outside context. Such a replacement would preserve the baseline and target avoidance and contradict minimality.

declarationGroups.title: Boundary and replacement theorems
declarationGroups.explanation: These declarations separate unequal boundary-degree fibres, prove universal context response, derive global graph obligations from literal gluing, and conclude hereditary target-uncompressibility.

  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        left ≠
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.BoundaryDegreeProfile boundaries
        right →
    ¬StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.TargetComplete input boundaries
        left right

declarations.type:

      left right

  replacement.LexSmaller source →
    (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries replacement outside).lexRank <
      (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries source outside).lexRank
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue_lexRank_lt

declarations.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T) [Nonempty T] (threshold : ℕ)
  (source replacement : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T)
  (outside : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T),
  (∀ (boundary : T),
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries replacement boundary =
        StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.boundaryDegree boundaries source boundary) →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece.InternalBaseline boundaries threshold replacement →

proofSteps.workBound: Zero node-local scans: one proof-level exact dichotomy and the isolated HSS theorem; no path, tuple, graph, or context universe is materialized.
proofSteps.title: Forced induced P₁₃
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The corollary is fully formalized relative to the explicitly isolated HSS theorem. The app must display that trust boundary separately from kernel-proved consequences.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: CT1 tests for an induced P₁₃. Its avoiding residual is literal P₁₃-freeness, which the Hegde--Sandeep--Shashank theorem turns into a forbidden power-of-two cycle. Therefore the minimal counterexample contains an induced P₁₃, recorded by a proof-carrying graph embedding.

declarationGroups.title: Trusted external closure
declarationGroups.explanation: This isolated axiom is exactly the cited Hegde--Sandeep--Shashank theorem: finite P₁₃-free minimum-degree-three graphs contain a power-of-two cycle. It is the sole trusted external theorem in the repository.

declarations.type:
∀ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) [inst : Fintype V] [inst_1 : DecidableRel G.Adj],
  3 ≤ G.minDegree →
    StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathFree G 13 →
      StructuralExhaustion.Graph.HasCycleWithLength G fun length => ∃ exponent, 2 ≤ exponent ∧ length = 2 ^ exponent
declarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.External.HegdeSandeepShashank.p13Free_hasPowerOfTwoCycle

proofSteps.title: Maximal induced-P₁₃ packing
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This descriptor covers only node [17]. Remainder construction and every quantitative packing consequence belong to later diagram nodes and are not front-loaded here.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: On node [16]'s literal not-P₁₃-free survivor, CT12 selects a deterministic vertex-disjoint induced-P₁₃ list and proves it maximal by saturation. The public node-[17] output deliberately makes no maximum-cardinality or remainder claim.
proofSteps.formalStatement: \mathcal P\text{ is vertex-disjoint and maximal among induced }P_{13}\text{ families}

declarationGroups.title: Node [17] maximal packing semantics
declarationGroups.explanation: The selected list is nonempty, support-nodup, pairwise disjoint, and saturated: every induced P₁₃ meets a selected window. Saturation is the diagram's maximality assertion and does not imply or expose maximum cardinality.

          13 window)
        (StructuralExhaustion.Graph.InducedPathMaximalPacking.support (Internal.Node17Context stage.previous).G.object
          13 selected)

proofSteps.title: Exact P₁₃ attachment-label algebra
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [18]'s legal-label table and Cₛ/Ω₂ definitions are proved. Node [21]'s 91-barrier interface is tracked separately and is kernel-checked through its packed-row semantics, safe/flat count obligations, and sole-HSS-clean evidence.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: An outside vertex is labelled by the path positions to which it is adjacent. Target avoidance forbids pairs of positions at gaps two and six. Two parity automata symbolically count the seven size fibres and transport the exact 399-row carrier into CT10; no global code scan is used for the count.

proofSteps.title: Conditional nodes [22]--[24] exact density branch
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Nodes [22]--[24] consume node [21]'s kernel-checked accumulated stage. Node [23] is the exact local continuation of node [22]'s yes constructor and accepts no quiet-block premise. Node [24]'s scalar transformer and accumulated hot-certificate StageEntails instances are kernel-checked; node [52] separately supplies the later joint-budget premise on its own branch.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: From the exact typed node-[21] output, node [22] compares the fixed-point window and skeleton loads. Node [23] exposes the strict overflow on the literal yes constructor without assuming it is empty. Node [24] retains the complementary no constructor, window cap, packing support, and a reusable theorem converting the later joint high-entropy budget into the sharper cap.

declarationGroups.title: Literal framework decisions and reusable node-[24] transformer
declarationGroups.explanation: Core owns both decisions, the strict-leaf closure, the surviving active cursor, and the single accumulated ledger. Node [22] consumes the literal node-[21] ledger output without an application-owned handoff.

proofSteps.workBound: The explicit coordinate schedule contains exactly W₂(R) and at most n³ actual wedges. The maximum is a proof-level bounded cardinality definition: no subfamily powerset, quotient family, outside-context family, support family, path family, subgraph family, or graph universe is evaluated.
proofSteps.title: Curvature target-rank on the exact wedge family
proofSteps.scopeNotes: The local node-[31] rank construction is kernel-checked, but the node remains partial through inherited missing obligations.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: The raw coordinate family consists exactly of a remainder center and a canonical unordered pair of its internal neighbours, so its cardinality is W₂(R). The public rank is now the attained maximum over every carrier-indexed functional admissible quotient of the exact graph response profile.
proofSteps.formalStatement: r_\Omega(R)=\max\{|\mathcal A|:\mathcal A\subseteq\mathcal W_2(R),\ \mathcal A\text{ survives every functional admissible quotient}\}

proofSteps.title: High-entropy remainder bit contribution
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [51] is only the high-branch arithmetic handoff. It feeds the original node-[52] window-plus-remainder accounting step; it does not prove window/remainder independence or the downstream entropy cap.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [51] consumes node [50]'s exact high natural-power proposition on the full accumulated residual. Monotonicity and the power law for base-two logarithms give the printed remainder-bit contribution by symbolic arithmetic.
proofSteps.formalStatement: n^{|R|}\le |\mathcal G_{\rm real}(R)|^{10}\Longrightarrow (|R|/10)\log_2 n\le\log_2|\mathcal G_{\rm real}(R)|

        {outerYes outerNo : (residual : Residual) → Active residual → Prop} →
          {OuterYesOutput : (residual : Residual) → (data : Active residual) → outerYes residual data → Type uStage} →
            {innerYes innerNo : (residual : Residual) → (data : Active residual) → outerNo residual data → Prop} →
              {Terminal :
                  (residual : Residual) →
                    (data : Active residual) →
                      (proof : outerYes residual data) → OuterYesOutput residual data proof → Type uOccurrence} →
                {guard :
                    (residual : Residual) →
                      (data : Active residual) →
                        (outerProof : outerNo residual data) → innerYes residual data outerProof → Prop} →
                  [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.Proofs.Contains
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available
                          (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchYesContinuationNoDecision
                            Bypass Active outerYes outerNo OuterYesOutput innerYes innerNo))
                        facts] →
                    ((residual : Residual) →
                        (data : Active residual) →
                          (proof : outerYes residual data) →
                            (output : OuterYesOutput residual data proof) → Terminal residual data proof output) →
                      ((residual : Residual) →
                          (data : Active residual) →
                            (outerProof : outerNo residual data) →
                              (innerProof : innerYes residual data outerProof) →
                                Decidable (guard residual data outerProof innerProof)) →
                        (∀ (residual : Residual) (data : Active residual) (outerProof : outerNo residual data)
                            (innerProof : innerYes residual data outerProof),
                            guard residual data outerProof innerProof → False) →
                          StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.StageNode

It compares the exact forced curvature power with the flat curvature power times the inherited low-entropy allowance, retaining both manuscript edges. The high constructor and node [52] output pass through unchanged.

{Residual : Type uResidual} →
  {facts : List (Residual → Prop)} →
    {Bypass : Residual → Type uInput} →
      {Active : Residual → Type uTarget} →
        {yes no : (residual : Residual) → Active residual → Prop} →
          {Output : (residual : Residual) → (data : Active residual) → no residual data → Sort uStage} →
            {nextYes nextNo :
                (residual : Residual) →
                  StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive Active no
                      Output residual →
                    Prop} →
              [StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.Proofs.Contains
                    (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.Available
                      (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuation Bypass
                        Active yes no Output))
                    facts] →
                ((residual : Residual) →
                    (active :
                        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive
                          Active no Output residual) →
                      Decidable (nextYes residual active)) →
                  (∀ (residual : Residual)
                      (active :
                        StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive
                          Active no Output residual),
                      ¬nextYes residual active → nextNo residual active) →
                    StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.StageNode
                      (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecision
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationBypass
                          Bypass Active yes no)
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.ResidualRefinement.State.FocusedBranchDecisionNoContinuationActive
                          Active no Output)
                        nextYes nextNo)

proofSteps.title: Ambient-cubic cold handoff
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [151] is the local ambient-cubic handoff for cold windows; later bounded-germ work starts after this handoff.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [151] partitions the literal cold-window list into ambient-cubic and non-cubic windows and bounds the non-cubic loss by the inherited near-cubic surplus budget.
proofSteps.formalStatement: C_{\rm cubic}+C_{\rm noncubic}=C_{\rm cold},\qquad C_{\rm noncubic}^{2}\le B_{\rm surplus}

declarationGroups.title: Cubic/non-cubic cold partition
declarationGroups.explanation: The node works only with the cold-window list carried by node [150]; it proves the partition, duplicate-free cubic list, non-cubic surplus bound, and square-loss bound.

proofSteps.workBound: One graph-owned first-failure run per scheduled branch-excess stub; no graph, context, state-family, assignment, or Boolean universe is enumerated.
proofSteps.title: Cold-corridor first-failure scan
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [153] is green because its local scan and residual handoff are implemented. The residual requirements it outputs are node [154]'s frontier, not unfinished node-[153] work.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [153] runs the graph-owned first-failure corridor machine on each node-[152] scheduled stub, proves the scan count, and carries the exact residual requirements to node [154].

declarationGroups.title: Graph-owned first-failure scan
declarationGroups.explanation: The graph layer supplies the corridor producer and totality theorem. The Erdős node schedules the carried branch-excess stubs, records hit/germ results, and exposes only the residual requirements prescribed for node [154].

proofSteps.title: Cold G1 target split
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [154] proves only the G1/no-G1 split. The no branch deliberately remains a no-F1 residual for nodes [156] and [157].
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [154] consumes the exact node-[153] first-failure residual and uses the framework focused-branch decision to split on whether a scheduled cold corridor has an F1 dyadic target hit.

declarationGroups.title: Framework G1 split
declarationGroups.explanation: The node defines G1 by the graph-owned F1 predicate on the carried branch-excess schedule, executes the framework focused decision, and continues the no branch as an exact no-F1 residual for the next G2/G3 split.

proofSteps.title: Cold G1 dyadic-cycle terminal
proofSteps.scopeNotes: Node [155] closes only the G1 terminal edge. The no-G1 cold residual remains with node [154]'s no continuation for later G2/G3 routing.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [155] closes exactly the G1 yes leaf by turning the framework F1 hit into the graph-owned power-of-two cycle target and contradicting the inherited target-avoidance theorem.

declarationGroups.title: Framework G1 closure
declarationGroups.explanation: The node consumes only the G1 yes leaf, obtains the target cycle with the graph-owned F1-to-target conversion, records the existing CT1 run certificate, and closes the branch through the framework focused-branch terminal combinator.

proofSteps.title: Cold G2/G3 split
proofSteps.scopeNotes: This is the verified G2/G3 split below no-G1. It does not yet close the G2 event branch, so node [156] remains outside the whole-node green list.
proofSteps.plainExplanation: Node [156] consumes the exact no-G1 residual from node [154] and uses the framework focused-branch decision to split the same scheduled corridor stages into an F4 G2 event branch and a no-F4 G3 residual.

declarationGroups.title: Framework G2/G3 split
declarationGroups.explanation: The node defines G2 by the graph-owned F4 predicate on the predecessor's scheduled corridor stage lists. The framework decides that focused branch and continues the no branch as the exact G3 residual for node [157].

nodeObligations.title: N7-PRED
nodeObligations.statement: Consume the literal node-[6] certificate decision.

nodeObligations.title: N7-CYCLE
nodeObligations.statement: Interpret the stored C1 certificate as the official power-of-two-cycle target.

nodeObligations.title: N7-CLOSE
nodeObligations.statement: Contradict that target with the inherited minimal-counterexample avoidance theorem.

nodeObligations.title: N7-SURVIVOR
nodeObligations.statement: Retain the literal CT1 avoiding run as the sole live node-[6] successor.

nodeObligations.title: N8-PRED
nodeObligations.statement: Consume only node-[7]'s exact surviving CT1 avoiding successor.

nodeObligations.title: N8-CORE
nodeObligations.statement: Exclude every proper subgraph of minimum degree at least three by minimality.

nodeObligations.title: N8-CT2
nodeObligations.statement: Retain the canonical certified CT2 deletion terminal and trace for each supplied proper core.

nodeObligations.title: N8-WORK
nodeObligations.statement: Use one local certificate check and enumerate no subgraph family.

nodeObligations.title: N9-PRED
nodeObligations.statement: Consume the literal node-[8] stage.

nodeObligations.title: N9-DELETE
nodeObligations.statement: Apply the graph-owned deletion-criticality theorem on that selected graph.

nodeObligations.title: N9-DEG3
nodeObligations.statement: Prove every edge has a degree-three endpoint.

nodeObligations.title: N9-LEDGER
nodeObligations.statement: Append the endpoint theorem once to the accumulated ledger.

nodeObligations.title: N10-PRED

frameworkDeclaration.type:
{V : Type uVertex} →
  {BranchState : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.FiniteObject V → Type uBranch} →
    (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput V BranchState) →
      StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetCertificateEncoding input.Target
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.MinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.edgeRootedEncoding

interfaceBindings.description: A rooted return is the positive code and exact return-set disjointness supplies the avoiding execution.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
(input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) →
  (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target) →
    (subgraph : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedFiniteObject.ProperSubgraph ctx.G) →
      (baseline : input.problem.Baseline subgraph.value) →
        StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CertifiedReductionRun ctx (input.properSubgraphCT2Input ctx subgraph baseline)
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput.properSubgraphCT2Run

interfaceBindings.description: The application supplies one finite proper-subgraph certificate; the framework proves rank decrease, runs the canonical deletion-C2 path, and exposes a constant work bound.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
∀ (input : StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput) {T : Type u} (boundaries : FinEnum T)
  [inst : Nonempty T] (ctx : StructuralExhaustion.Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target),
  StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.VerifiedStage input boundaries ctx
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.verifiedStage

interfaceBindings.description: A local representative supplies boundary response, internal degree, and local smallness; literal gluing derives whole-rank decrease, baseline preservation, and target transport before the minimality contradiction.

frameworkDeclaration.type:
∀ {Candidate : Type uCandidate} (profile : StructuralExhaustion.CT10.ExhaustiveClassification.Profile Candidate)
  {P : StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem} (context : StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext P),
  profile.VerifiedStage context
frameworkDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT10.ExhaustiveClassification.Profile.verifiedStage

interfaceBindings.description: The problem supplies a compact thirteen-bit legality predicate proved equivalent to graph-theoretic cycle avoidance; the generic CT10 profile classifies the 8192 local candidates, audits the complete 399-row table, and retains a quadratic work certificate.
